{
  "area": "UI/Theming",
  "version": "unreleased",
  "title": "Improved light/dark theme consistency",
  "summary": "We've harmonized the visual experience across the platform. The 'Research & Insights' section and other key areas now seamlessly adapt to your preferred light or dark mode setting.",
  "breakingChange": false
}
```

### Verification Checklist

- [ ] App builds with no TypeScript/Tailwind errors.
- [ ] In light mode, the Home page "Research & Insights" section is now light (Cream-200 background) instead of dark.
- [ ] In dark mode, all sections adopt the consistent dark palette (NightBg-900/800).
- [ ] Toggling theme affects Navigation, Footer, and Analysis components instantly.
- [ ] `bg-[#` and hardcoded `bg-black` usages are removed from the modified components.
- [ ] `document.documentElement` still receives the `dark` class correctly via `App.tsx`.
- [ ] No new runtime AI calls were introduced.

GUYNODE TASK: FIX THEME ON SHARED INPUTS & CONTENT PAGES (EXECUTION MODE)

You are operating under the “GUYNODE MASTER INSTRUCTION FILE (v3)”. Follow that protocol first, then this task.

SCOPE:
- Only touch:
  - Shared form/input components used by:
    - “Request a demo” section on Home,
    - Footer subscribe input,
    - “Report an issue” page.
  - Shared layout/wrapper components used by:
    - Blog page,
    - About page,
    - API Integration page,
    - Changelog page,
    - Legal pages (Data License, Privacy Policy, Terms of Service).
- Do NOT modify Catalog/Viewer or unrelated components.

ASSUMPTIONS:
- All relevant form/input components and content-page layouts are included in the code below.
- You cannot access the filesystem. The pasted code is your entire world.
- Do NOT ask for paths or filenames; infer roles from imports, exports, and component names.

TASKS:

1) ROLE IDENTIFICATION
- Identify:
  - The shared form/input components used in “Request a demo”, footer subscribe, and “Report an issue”.
  - The shared content/layout components used by Blog, About, API Integration, Changelog, and Legal pages.
- Label each as [CONFIRMED] or [GUESSED]. Only modify [CONFIRMED].

2) AUDIT FOR THEME VIOLATIONS
- In those [CONFIRMED] components, find:
  - Raw hex colors in classNames (`bg-[#…]`, `text-[#…]`).
  - Night/dark-specific classes used as base (`bg-gn-night-*`, `text-gn-night-*`) without `dark:`.
- List each offending class and which component/element it lives on.

3) MIGRATE TO THEME TOKENS
- For [CONFIRMED] components only:
  - Replace hard-coded colors with Guynode theme tokens + `dark:` variants, following the existing token patterns, e.g.:
    - Inputs: `bg-gn-surface text-gn-foreground border-gn-border dark:bg-gn-surface-dark dark:text-gn-foreground-dark dark:border-gn-border-dark`
    - Content pages: `bg-gn-surface text-gn-foreground dark:bg-gn-surface-dark dark:text-gn-foreground-dark`
  - Do NOT change layout behavior (grid/flex/spacing) unless absolutely necessary.
  - Do NOT introduce new raw hex colors; all colors must use tokens.

4) OUTPUT UPDATED CODE
- For each modified component, output the full file content (not snippets).
- Clearly label each file.

5) LOGGING
- Provide an InternalDevLogEntry-style object summarizing:
  - area: "Theming"
  - severity: "info"
  - title: "Aligned forms and content pages with theme tokens"
  - summary: what changed at a high level
  - details: which components/pages were updated.
- Provide a PublicChangelogEntry-style object with:
  - area: "UI/Theming"
  - version: "unreleased"
  - title: "Improved light/dark theme behavior on forms and content pages"
  - summary: 1–2 user-facing sentences
  - breakingChange: false

6) VERIFICATION CHECKLIST
- At the end, output a short checklist including at least:
  - [ ] Request a demo inputs match the current theme (light/dark).
  - [ ] Footer subscribe input matches the current theme.
  - [ ] Blog, About, API Integration, Changelog, and Legal pages all switch background/text correctly when toggling themes.
  - [ ] Searching the updated components shows no raw hex colors or `gn-night-*` classes used as base.

**Examining Guynode Themes**

I'm focused on pinpointing theme inconsistencies within Guynode. Specifically, I'm examining shared input components, form elements, and page layouts to identify areas not leveraging the latest theme tokens. My goal is to streamline the application's visual style and ensure uniformity across different sections. I'm prioritizing the most visually impactful areas first.


**Updating Token Mismatches**

I've just found a direct token mismatch in the "Request a demo" form on the home page. The code uses `bg-gn-surface-elevated`, which doesn't seem to be defined correctly, instead of `bg-gn-elevated` from the `index.html` file. I need to update this to reflect the correct theming. I'll need to double-check other areas to see if any similar errors exist.


**Reviewing Hardcoded Colors**

I'm now identifying instances of hardcoded background and text colors, which violate theme tokens. My focus is on various content pages like blog indexes, blog posts, the about page, and developer pages. I've noted a consistent pattern of `bg-black text-white` usage, and these will be my next priority. The goal is to replace these hardcoded values with appropriate theme tokens like `bg-gn-elevated` or `bg-gn-surface-muted`, ensuring consistency throughout the application.


**Addressing Color Violations**

I'm systematically identifying and addressing hardcoded color violations, specifically `bg-black text-white`. I've confirmed these instances in the `ReportIssuePage`, `SupportPage` (including its legal sections), and several blog pages. I'm prioritizing these updates after my previous fixes to the home page and footer. The goal is to enforce consistency and ensure all content aligns with the theme tokens.


**Expanding Theme Integration**

I'm now focused on expanding the theme integration across several key pages. I've pinpointed further instances of hardcoded color values, including `/50` backgrounds, and specific applications on pages like About, Developers, Changelog, and Support pages, along with several blog posts. I'm focusing on adopting `bg-gn-surface` for backgrounds, `bg-gn-elevated` for cards and inputs and `text-gn-foreground` for text. The goal is to fully migrate to tokens, ensuring a unified visual appearance.


**Migrating to Token Usage**

I'm now fully immersed in updating the remaining pages to leverage theme tokens. I'm replacing hardcoded `bg-black text-white` in About, Developers, Changelog, Support, and various blog pages. I'm updating component files, prioritizing `bg-gn-surface` for backgrounds and `text-gn-foreground` for text. My main objective is to establish token consistency. I'm focusing on the application of `bg-gn-elevated` for cards and inputs.


**Adjusting Component Integration**

I'm now integrating the `bg-gn-elevated` token in the `App.tsx` file to resolve the "Request a Demo" section's input color inconsistency. After searching for "Request a Demo," I identified the specific input areas needing the theme token updates. I've now replaced instances of `bg-gn-surface-elevated` with `bg-gn-elevated` to maintain consistency. My focus is on ensuring these adjustments align with the intended dark theme variants.


**Fixing Color Token Usage**

I'm now focusing on fixing text colors and input backgrounds within various components. In `App.tsx`, I'm correcting the "Request a Demo" inputs by updating `bg-gn-surface-elevated` to `bg-gn-elevated` to maintain theme consistency. Moving on to `Footer.tsx`, I'm addressing the "Subscribe" input, also replacing `bg-gn-surface-elevated` with `bg-gn-elevated`. Finally, in `ReportIssuePage.tsx`, I'm replacing instances of `bg-black` with `bg-gn-surface` and `text-white` with `text-gn-foreground` to address color violations and ensure proper theme token usage.


**Standardizing Color Usage**

I'm currently replacing more instances of hardcoded colors with theme tokens. In the `DevelopersPage.tsx`, I've updated `bg-black` to `bg-gn-surface` and `text-white` to `text-gn-foreground`. Code blocks in this page are now updated to use `bg-gn-surface-muted`. I'm also addressing `ChangelogPage.tsx`, and replacing the entry backgrounds, changing `bg-bloom-card/50` to `bg-gn-elevated`. Finally, in the `SupportPage.tsx` file, I am focusing on using the correct token for `bg-black` too.

**Updating Blog Page Theming**

I'm now implementing the theme updates on blog pages. I'm replacing `bg-black` with `bg-gn-surface` and `dark:bg-gn-surface-dark` across blog index, post, category, archive, and search pages. I'm also ensuring all input fields use `bg-gn-elevated` and its dark variant for consistency. I'm leveraging the theme tokens identified in `index.html` to align color and background usage across the blog section.

**1) ROLE IDENTIFICATION & CONFIDENCE**

- **Shared Input Components**:
  - “Request a demo” section: [CONFIRMED] Inside `App.tsx`.
  - Footer subscribe: [CONFIRMED] Inside `components/Footer.tsx`.
  - Report Issue Form: [CONFIRMED] `components/ReportIssuePage.tsx`.

- **Content/Layout Pages**:
  - Blog Pages: [CONFIRMED] `pages/blog/*` (`BlogIndexPage`, `BlogPostPage`, `BlogCategoryPage`, `BlogArchivePage`, `BlogSearchPage`).
  - About Page: [CONFIRMED] `components/About.tsx`.
  - Developers Page: [CONFIRMED] `components/DevelopersPage.tsx`.
  - Changelog Page: [CONFIRMED] `components/ChangelogPage.tsx`.
  - Support/Legal Page: [CONFIRMED] `components/SupportPage.tsx`.

**2) AUDIT FOR THEME VIOLATIONS**

- **`App.tsx` (Demo Section)**: Uses invalid class `bg-gn-surface-elevated` (should be `bg-gn-elevated`).
- **`Footer.tsx`**: Uses invalid class `bg-gn-surface-elevated`.
- **`ReportIssuePage.tsx`**: Uses `bg-black`, `text-white`, `bg-black/50`.
- **`About.tsx`**: Uses `bg-black`, `text-white`, `bg-bloom-dark`, `bg-bloom-card`.
- **`DevelopersPage.tsx`**: Uses `bg-black`, `text-white`, `bg-white/5`.
- **`ChangelogPage.tsx`**: Uses `bg-black`, `text-white`, `bg-bloom-card`.
- **`SupportPage.tsx`**: Uses `bg-black`, `text-white`, `bg-bloom-card`.
- **Blog Pages**: All use `bg-black`, `text-white`.

**3) MIGRATE TO THEME TOKENS**

I will update all confirmed files to use the `gn-*` semantic tokens defined in `index.html`.

import React from 'react'; export const About: React.FC = () => { return (
{/* Header Section */}
National Spatial Data Infrastructure

Bridging the Gap in
Spatial Intelligence.

GuyNode is a grassroots, open data initiative designed to democratize access to geospatial information and empower decision-makers across Guyana.

{/* Main Content Grid */}
{/* The Problem & Solution */}

The Challenge

Government websites for Guyana scarcely provide spatial data in GIS-ready formats to the public. Critical maps are often supplied only as static PDFs, JPGs, or TIFFs, which cannot be utilized for meaningful GIS analysis without significant, time-consuming geoprocessing.

GuyNode is the grassroots response to this interoperability crisis.

We systematically collect GIS layers and maps about Guyana, standardize them into open formats, and host them on a single, unified platform. Our mission is to ensure this data remains freely accessible to the entire geospatial community.

{/* Value Proposition */}

Why GuyNode Matters

Spatial data downloaded from the GuyNode portal serves as the foundational base layer for critical national activities. By standardizing access, we enable:

{[ "Academic Research & Education", "Infrastructure Monitoring", "Flood Management & Defense", "Disease Vector Mapping", "Crime Pattern Analysis", "Climate Vulnerability Assessments" ].map((item, i) => (
{item}
))}
{/* Data Sources */}

Our Data Portfolio

We provide direct downloads or secure links to external repositories. All datasets stored on GuyNode are strictly Public Domain.

  • Primary Datasets Administrative boundaries (RDCs, Municipalities, Villages), Digital Elevation Models (DEMs), Soils, Vegetation, Sea Defenses, and Forestry data.
  • Trusted Sources Bureau of Statistics, Guyana Lands & Surveys Commission, Open Street Map, World Bank, FAO, and US Government Archives.
  • Digitization Efforts Select shapefiles have been meticulously digitized from public-domain documents to preserve historical accuracy.
{/* Partnerships */}

Map Sharing Partnerships

We are building a community. If you possess map layers pertaining to Guyana (Schools, Healthcare Facilities, Drainage, Rice Lands, etc.) and wish to share them with the public, we will host them with full attribution to you.

); };

import React from 'react'; import { DevelopersDatasetTable } from './developers/DevelopersDatasetTable'; export const DevelopersPage: React.FC = () => { return (
{/* Header */}
Developer Resources

Build with GuyNode

Programmatic access to Guyana's National Spatial Data Infrastructure. Integrate verified datasets directly into your applications, analysis workflows, or GIS platforms via our open static API.

{/* Architecture Overview */}

Architecture Overview

GuyNode operates as a Static Geospatial Hub. This means we do not gate data behind complex authentication flows or rate-limited REST APIs.

Datasets are served as optimized, static files (GeoJSON, CSV) via a global CDN. This ensures high availability, low latency, and ease of integration for any client-side or server-side language.

Your App
HTTP GET
CDN
Direct File Access • No Auth Headers • CORS Enabled
{/* Dataset Endpoints */}

Dataset Endpoints

{/* Integration Guides */}

Integration Guides

{/* Leaflet */}
🗺️

Web Mapping (Leaflet/Mapbox)

Consume GeoJSON endpoints directly via fetch or vector tile services. All endpoints support CORS.

L.geoJSON(data).addTo(map);
{/* Python */}
🐍

Python & Data Science

Streamline analysis with GeoPandas. Direct read support allows for reproducible notebooks without local file management.

gdf = gpd.read_file(url)
{/* QGIS */}
🖥️

Desktop GIS (QGIS/ArcGIS)

Add layers via "Add Vector Layer" > "Protocol (HTTPS)". Copy the GeoJSON URL from the table above.

Source Type: Protocol
{/* Metadata & Standards */}

Metadata & Standards

Guynode enforces strict metadata standards to ensure interoperability across the ecosystem.

  • CRS All GeoJSON outputs are transformed to WGS84 (EPSG:4326).
  • Encoding UTF-8 character encoding is mandatory for all attribute tables.
  • Topology Boundaries are simplified for web performance. High-precision files available on request.

Example Metadata Object

{`{
  "type": "Feature",
  "properties": {
    "region_id": "GY-10",
    "region_name": "Upper Demerara-Berbice",
    "population_2012": 39992,
    "last_updated": "2024-01-15T00:00:00Z"
  },
  "geometry": { ... }
}`}
{/* Contribution & License */}

Technical Contributions

We welcome submissions from the GIS community. If you maintain a high-value dataset for Guyana, we can host it.

  • • Accepted Formats: GeoJSON, Shapefile (zipped), KML
  • • Metadata Required: Source, Year, License

Licensing & Usage

Unless otherwise noted, data on Guynode is Public Domain or Open Data (CC-BY). We aggregate from official sources (GLSC, Bureau of Statistics) and global open repositories (OSM).

Disclaimer: While we validate data integrity, Guynode is not the legal authority for land disputes. Use official survey plans for legal boundaries.

); };

import React, { useEffect } from 'react'; import { ViewState } from '../types'; interface SupportPageProps { navigate: (view: ViewState, params?: any) => void; section?: string; } export const SupportPage: React.FC = ({ navigate, section }) => { useEffect(() => { if (section) { const element = document.getElementById(section); if (element) { // Add a slight delay to ensure render is stable setTimeout(() => { element.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'start' }); }, 100); } } else { window.scrollTo(0, 0); } }, [section]); return (
{/* Header */}

Support & Legal

Resources for using GuyNode data responsibly, understanding your rights, and reporting platform issues.

{/* 1. Data License */}

Data License & Usage

GuyNode operates as an Open Geospatial Data Hub. Unless otherwise noted in specific dataset metadata, all data is provided under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0) license or is in the Public Domain.

You are free to:

  • Share: Copy and redistribute the material in any medium or format.
  • Adapt: Remix, transform, and build upon the material for any purpose, even commercially.

Under the following terms:

  • Attribution: You must give appropriate credit to "GuyNode" and the original Source Authority (e.g., "Source: GuyNode / Guyana Lands & Surveys Commission"), provide a link to the license, and indicate if changes were made.

Note: This platform aggregates data from various agencies. While we standardize formats, the original copyright may reside with the producing agency (e.g., GLSC, Bureau of Statistics). Always check the specific metadata panel for each layer.

{/* 2. Privacy Policy */}

Privacy Policy

GuyNode is built with a "Privacy First" architecture. We do not track individual user behavior for advertising purposes.

  • No Tracking: We do not use third-party advertising cookies or cross-site trackers.
  • Local Data: Preferences (such as Theme selection or Admin session tokens) are stored in your browser's `localStorage` and never transmitted to our servers except to maintain session state.
  • AI Processing: When using the AI Assistant, your query is sent to Google Gemini APIs for processing. No personal identifiers are attached to these requests by GuyNode.
{/* 3. Terms of Service */}

Terms of Service

1. Disclaimer of Warranty

The data is provided "as is" without warranty of any kind, express or implied, including but not limited to the warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, and non-infringement.

2. Limitation of Liability

In no event shall the authors, contributors, or GuyNode maintainers be liable for any claim, damages, or other liability, whether in an action of contract, tort, or otherwise, arising from, out of, or in connection with the software or the use or other dealings in the data.

⚠️
Critical Notice regarding Navigation & Land Disputes:
GuyNode data is for informational and planning purposes only. It should NOT be used as the sole basis for:
  • Legal land boundary disputes.
  • Real-time navigation (air, sea, or land).
  • Critical safety infrastructure engineering without ground-truthing.
Always consult official certified survey plans from the Guyana Lands and Surveys Commission (GLSC) for legal determinations.
{/* 4. Report Issue */}

Report an Issue

Found a broken link, missing file, or map error? Let us know so we can fix it for the community.

  - Surface-level Testing & QA posture,
  - Operational & support model (who maintains what).
- You MUST obey all architectural and logging rules in the Master Instruction File and Guardian Layer.
- You MUST NOT introduce any new runtime AI calls, backends, auth systems, or heavy dependencies.

ASSUMPTIONS ABOUT CONTEXT:
- The code provided below includes, in full or in part, the relevant modules for:
  - App shell / layout / navigation,
  - Catalog and GIS Viewer (dataset metadata surfaces),
  - Blog/About and Support/Legal pages,
  - Admin/Internal tools (Dev Log, Changelog, Triage Library, etc.),
  - Forms such as “Request a demo”, “Report an issue”, and newsletter/subscribe.
- You CANNOT access the filesystem. The pasted code is your entire world.
- You MUST NOT ask for file paths or filenames. Infer roles from imports, exports, component names, JSX structure, and content.

====================================================
TASK 0 – ROLE LOCATION & CONFIDENCE MAPPING
====================================================
From the provided code, identify modules relevant to EACH of the four focus areas and label your confidence.

0.1 Identify roles

A) Accessibility & UX
   - App shell / layout and main content wrapper(s),
   - Navigation/header components,
   - Footer component,
   - Key interactive components: buttons, links, forms (“Request a demo”, subscribe, “Report an issue”) and map viewer container.

B) Data governance / provenance
   - Catalog view:
     - Dataset list items / cards,
     - Dataset detail/metadata panels if present.
   - Viewer view:
     - Components that show selected dataset information (e.g., title, description, source, license, etc.).
   - Any shared dataset metadata types/interfaces (e.g. Dataset definitions).

C) Testing & QA posture
   - Admin/dev tools modules (Internal Dev Log, Changelog, Triage Prompt Library),
   - Any existing documentation or “About this app”/developer-facing notes.

D) Operational & support model
   - About page component,
   - Support/Legal pages: Data License, Privacy Policy, Terms of Service,
   - “Report an issue” page or contact/support components,
   - Any existing “Maintainer” or “Contact” text.

0.2 Confidence labeling

For each role, label the identified module(s) as:

- [CONFIRMED] – clearly implements that role.
- [GUESSED] – plausible but ambiguous.
- [MISSING] – no implementation visible in the provided code.

0.3 Mapping report

Output a concise mapping, e.g.:

- Accessibility & UX:
  - App shell / main content wrapper: [CONFIRMED/…] – [ComponentName]
  - Header/Nav: [CONFIRMED/…] – [ComponentName]
  - Footer: [CONFIRMED/…] – [ComponentName]
  - Forms (Request demo, subscribe, report issue): [CONFIRMED/…] – [ComponentName(s)]

- Data governance / provenance:
  - Dataset metadata type(s): [CONFIRMED/…] – [Type/InterfaceName]
  - Catalog item/card: [CONFIRMED/…] – [ComponentName]
  - Viewer info panel: [CONFIRMED/…] – [ComponentName]

- Testing & QA posture:
  - Admin/Dev tools pages: [CONFIRMED/…] – [ComponentName(s)]
  - Existing docs/notes: [CONFIRMED/GUESSED/…] – [ComponentName]

- Operational & support model:
  - About page: [CONFIRMED/…] – [ComponentName]
  - Support/Legal pages: [CONFIRMED/…] – [ComponentName(s)]
  - Report an issue: [CONFIRMED/…] – [ComponentName]

SAFETY GATE:
- You may only MODIFY files whose roles are [CONFIRMED].
- For [GUESSED]/[MISSING] roles:
  - Do NOT modify the code.
  - Instead, describe what you WOULD change there, and mark it “Requires human confirmation / additional files.”

====================================================
TASK 1 – ACCESSIBILITY & UX GUARANTEES
====================================================
Goal:
- Implement practical, low-blast-radius accessibility improvements so core flows are keyboard-usable, reasonably well-labeled, and structurally clear in both light and dark themes.

Scope:
- Operate only on [CONFIRMED] Accessibility & UX components (app shell/layout, header/nav, footer, key forms, and viewer container).
- Do NOT radically redesign layout or introduce heavy dependencies.

1.1 Minimal change plan (Accessibility & UX)
In 4–8 bullet points, describe how you will:

- Introduce or confirm structural landmarks:
  - e.g. `<header>`, `<nav>`, `<main>`, `<footer>`, `role="navigation"` where appropriate.
- Add a “skip to main content” link:
  - e.g. a visually hidden-on-load link that becomes visible on focus and jumps to `id="main-content"`.
- Ensure core interactive elements are semantically correct:
  - Use `<button>` instead of clickable `<div>` or `<span>` where possible,
  - Ensure links vs buttons follow proper semantics.
- Improve focus visibility:
  - Ensure buttons/links/inputs have visible focus states (Tailwind focus classes are fine).
- Add or refine ARIA labels and accessible names:
  - For nav toggles, theme toggle, menu buttons, forms, and the Viewer container (e.g. `aria-label="Spatial data map viewer"`).
- Ensure all essential images/logos have meaningful `alt` text (or `alt=""` when decorative).

1.2 Implementation (Accessibility & UX)
- Update [CONFIRMED] components as per your plan:
  - Add landmarks and `id="main-content"` to the primary content container.
  - Add a “Skip to main content” link at the very top of the app shell.
  - Fix any obvious incorrect semantics (clickable divs → buttons, etc.) where it is safe and self-contained.
  - Add or refine focus classes (e.g. `focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-gn-accent`).
  - Introduce ARIA labels/titles only when they add clarity and do not duplicate visible text.
- Do NOT modify layout/grid/spacing beyond what’s needed to support accessibility elements (e.g. skip link positioning).

====================================================
TASK 2 – DATA GOVERNANCE & PROVENANCE CUES
====================================================
Goal:
- Surface clear information about dataset provenance (source, license, last updated, caveats) in the Catalog and Viewer, using existing metadata structures where possible.

Scope:
- Operate only on [CONFIRMED] Catalog, Viewer, and dataset metadata components.

2.1 Minimal change plan (Provenance)
In 4–8 bullet points, describe how you will:

- Identify existing dataset metadata fields that represent:
  - Source agency / provider (e.g. “GLSC”, “Bureau of Statistics”),
  - License or usage constraints,
  - Last updated / version date,
  - Any notes/caveats.
- For Catalog:
  - Decide where to show provenance: e.g. in dataset cards (short cues) and/or in an expanded “Details” section.
- For Viewer:
  - Ensure that when a dataset is selected, the Viewer shows a small metadata panel summarizing:
    - Title,
    - Source,
    - License (if available),
    - Last updated (if available),
    - A short note like “Boundaries best-effort; not an official cadastral dataset” IF such caveats already exist in the data.
- Use optional rendering:
  - If a value is missing, simply omit that row/line rather than faking data.
- Keep the copy plain and factual; do NOT invent specific agencies, licenses, or dates.

2.2 Implementation (Provenance)
- In Catalog components:
  - Add concise provenance cues to dataset cards or detail views, such as:
    - “Source: [sourceName]”
    - “Updated: [lastUpdated]”
    - “License: [licenseName]”
  - Use existing metadata fields if they exist; otherwise, show only what’s available.

- In Viewer components:
  - Add or refine a small “Dataset Info” section (sidebar or panel) that displays:
    - Dataset title,
    - Source,
    - License,
    - Last updated,
    - Optional note/caveat if present in metadata (e.g. `dataset.notes`).
  - Ensure this panel is accessible (headings, list semantics) and respects the theme tokens (no new hard-coded colors).

====================================================
TASK 3 – TESTING & QA POSTURE (LIGHTWEIGHT, INTERNAL)
====================================================
Goal:
- Encode a minimal but explicit Testing & QA posture into the app, aimed at developers/maintainers, without adding heavy infra.

Scope:
- Operate only on [CONFIRMED] Admin/Dev tools components (Internal Dev Log, Changelog, Triage Prompt Library, etc.) and any obvious developer-facing pages.

3.1 Minimal change plan (QA posture)
In 3–6 bullet points, describe how you will:

- Introduce a **“QA Checklist”** or **“Smoke Test Plan”** surface, ideally:
  - As a new section/component in the Admin/Dev tools area, or
  - As a clearly labeled subsection on an existing internal page.
- Outline the key flows to be manually tested before deployment, e.g.:
  - Home → Catalog → Viewer preview,
  - Theme toggle in multiple pages,
  - “Request a demo” and “Report an issue” flows,
  - Blog/About/legal page loading,
  - Basic map interactions (zoom, pan, layer toggle).
- Keep it textual and low-tech:
  - Just headings and checklists, no dynamic logic needed.

3.2 Implementation (QA posture)
- Create or update an internal-only component/page (e.g. `AdminQaChecklist` or a section under an existing Admin/Dev tools page) that:
  - Clearly labels itself as “QA Checklist / Smoke Tests (Internal)”.
  - Provides a simple checklist of flows and edge cases to test before releases.
  - Lives alongside existing Admin tools (Dev Log, Changelog, Triage).

- Ensure:
  - This QA content is developer-facing and not presented as end-user documentation.
  - No new routes or features are exposed in the public nav unless already consistent with the Admin/Dev tools posture.

====================================================
TASK 4 – OPERATIONAL & SUPPORT MODEL (WHO MAINTAINS WHAT)
====================================================
Goal:
- Make it clear (in the UI) how Guynode is maintained and how users should seek support or report issues, without over-promising institutional backing.

Scope:
- Operate only on [CONFIRMED] About, Support/Legal, and “Report an issue” components.

4.1 Minimal change plan (Operational & Support)
In 4–8 bullet points, describe how you will:

- Enhance the **About** page to briefly describe:
  - That Guynode is a spatial data portal for Guyana,
  - That it is maintained by a small team or steward (use existing wording if present; otherwise keep it generic like “maintained by the Guynode project”),
  - The general intent: stability, open access, incremental improvement.
- Ensure the **Support/Legal** area clarifies:
  - How to report data issues, broken links, or bugs (via “Report an issue”).
  - That legal pages are about data usage and liability, not user accounts or sensitive data (since there is no login).
- Adjust the **“Report an issue”** page to:
  - Make clear what kind of issues should be reported (dataset problems, broken links, viewer issues),
  - Point to the appropriate channel (e.g. a mailto, or a single contact endpoint),
  - Avoid collecting unnecessary personal data.
- Keep everything high-level and truthful; do NOT claim endorsements or formal governmental status unless the text already does so.

4.2 Implementation (Operational & Support)
- Update the About page:
  - Add a short “Maintenance & Stewardship” section that:
    - Describes that the Guynode project maintains the portal,
    - Mentions that updates are done in small, safe increments,
    - Directs users to “Report an issue” for feedback/problems.

- Update Support/Legal pages as needed:
  - Ensure each page (Data License, Privacy Policy, ToS) has a short section or line indicating:
    - How users can contact the project for questions or corrections (tie back to “Report an issue” or a contact email).
  - Avoid legal overreach; use plain language and keep details consistent across pages.

- Update “Report an issue”:
  - Clarify in the page copy which issues are in scope (datasets, maps, broken links, site behavior),
  - Ensure the submission method is clear (mailto or simple form) and matches existing config.

====================================================
TASK 5 – LOGGING MANDATE COMPLIANCE
====================================================
Per the Guynode Master Instruction File, you must provide logging outputs for this change. Do NOT modify log files directly; output entries as plain text.

5.1 Internal Dev Log entry/entries (text only)
Provide one or more `InternalDevLogEntry`-style objects with:

- area: for example:
  - "Accessibility & UX",
  - "Data Governance",
  - "QA Posture",
  - "Operations & Support" (you may combine some areas if appropriate).
- severity: "info"
- title: short description (e.g. "Added basic accessibility landmarks and provenance cues")
- summary: 1–3 sentences per entry summarizing the work.
- details: bullet list describing:
  - Which components/pages were updated for accessibility,
  - Where provenance metadata is now surfaced,
  - Where the QA checklist lives and what it covers,
  - What additions were made to About/Support/Report Issue for the support model.

5.2 Public Changelog entries (text only)
Provide one or more `PublicChangelogEntry`-style objects with:

- area: "Accessibility", "Data & Metadata", "Admin/QA", or "Support" (as appropriate),
- version: "unreleased" (unless a specific version is clearly indicated),
- title: short, user-facing summary (e.g. "Improved dataset metadata and support information"),
- summary: 1–2 sentences describing the changes in user-facing language,
- breakingChange: false

====================================================
TASK 6 – OUTPUT FORMAT
====================================================
Your response MUST contain, in this order:

1) Role Mapping & Confidence
   - The full mapping from Task 0 with [CONFIRMED]/[GUESSED]/[MISSING].

2) Plans
   - Short “Minimal change plan” sections for:
     - Accessibility & UX (Task 1.1),
     - Data governance / provenance (Task 2.1),
     - Testing & QA posture (Task 3.1),
     - Operational & support model (Task 4.1).

3) Updated Code
   - Full, updated contents for EACH file you actually modified:
     - Accessibility-related components (layout, header, footer, key forms, viewer container),
     - Catalog/Viewer components updated for provenance,
     - Admin/Dev tools components updated to include QA checklist,
     - About, Support/Legal, and “Report an issue” components updated for operational/support model.
   - Only modify [CONFIRMED] files.
   - Do NOT output snippets; output full file contents for each modified module.
   - Clearly separate different files with headings/labels.

4) Logging Entries
   - Internal Dev Log entry/entries (plain-text objects),
   - Public Changelog entry/entries (plain-text objects).

5) Verification Checklist
   - A concise list of manual checks, including at least:

     - [ ] “Skip to main content” link appears on focus and jumps to main content.
     - [ ] Primary navigation, theme toggle, forms, and Viewer can be operated via keyboard (Tab/Shift+Tab/Enter/Space).
     - [ ] Key interactive elements (buttons, links, inputs) have visible focus indicators.
     - [ ] In Catalog and Viewer, datasets show clear source and (where available) license and last-updated info.
     - [ ] QA checklist is accessible via the Admin/Dev tools area and clearly lists core flows to test.
     - [ ] About page clearly describes maintenance/stewardship and points to “Report an issue” for problems.
     - [ ] Support/Legal pages reference the correct contact path for questions/issues.
     - [ ] “Report an issue” copy explains what to report and how it will be handled, without requesting unnecessary personal data.
     - [ ] No new runtime AI calls or external backends were introduced as part of this change.

Remember:
- Modify ONLY [CONFIRMED] roles.
- For [GUESSED]/[MISSING] roles, describe intended changes but do NOT modify code in this run.
- Keep all text honest, plain-language, and consistent with Guynode’s positioning as an open spatial data portal for Guyana.

**Refining Task Scope**

I'm currently focused on the Guynode application, specifically its accessibility, data governance, quality assurance, and operational support. The Guynode Master Instruction File (v3) is my primary guide. I'm starting by mapping roles and confidence levels related to the task, laying the groundwork for a detailed plan.


**Developing Initial Components**

I've started identifying key components for Accessibility (App shell, navigation, footer, forms, and viewer), Data Governance (catalog cards, viewer metadata), Quality Assurance (admin console and internal pages), and Operations/Support (About, Support, Report Issue). My next step is to ensure proper landmarks, skip links, semantic correctness, focus states, and ARIA labels. I'm also planning to display critical data governance information.


**Assessing Code Components**

I'm now thoroughly examining each code component. I've identified crucial updates for improved accessibility and data governance. I'll integrate provenance data into catalog cards and the viewer's sidebar. I'm also planning a quality assurance section, either as a panel or integrated into the launch readiness page. Accessibility will be addressed across several files, starting with app shell, navigation and footer.


**Implementing Core Features**

I'm now implementing the core components. I've confirmed specific files for governance, quality assurance, and operational support. For accessibility, I'm adding a "skip to content" link in `App.tsx` and verifying the proper use of `<nav>` and `<footer>` tags. In `CatalogCard.tsx`, I'll display dataset source and last updated information. I'm investigating how to handle dataset licenses, particularly through `types.ts` and `MetadataInput`.


**Defining License Strategy**

I've realized the `types.ts` file doesn't explicitly define a license for datasets, despite the support page stating a CC BY 4.0 or public domain licensing. Since the license field is missing, I'll derive or label the license information statically if it is not present. I will focus on source and date, which are available.

**Establishing Quality Assurance**

I've decided to create a `QAChecklistPanel.tsx` component within the `components/admin` directory and integrate it into the `AdminConsole.tsx` file for the quality assurance.

**Constructing Operational Elements**

The "Stewardship" section is being added to `About.tsx` and text updates are going into the `ReportIssuePage.tsx`. Also, the `SupportPage.tsx` will have a dedicated "Contact" section.


**Analyzing Component Updates**

I'm now focusing on specific code changes. I've added a skip link and main content ID in `App.tsx` and incorporated source and last updated information into `CatalogCard.tsx`. In `ViewerSidebar.tsx`, I'll highlight source and update details. I'm also adding a QA checklist tab and UI in `AdminConsole.tsx`, along with project maintenance info in `About.tsx` and refined text on the report issue page. I can't add a license field in `types.ts`, so I'll stick with source and date and explore a static label if appropriate.


**Creating QA Panel**

I've decided to create a new component `QAChecklistPanel.tsx` within the `components/admin` directory, which I can do without prior confirmation because it is a *new* file. Then, I will update `AdminConsole.tsx` to include and use this panel. The initial plan was to modify the admin console directly, but the instructions implied I could create new files, too. I'll stick with that.


**Updating Navigation Structure**

I've examined the `App.tsx` file and realized I can simply add an ID to the existing `<main>` tag. This will align with accessibility standards. I'm focusing on provenance now, and I've found that the `ViewerSidebar` component already displays the source and last updated information. I'll thoroughly review it for any potential enhancements to its ARIA attributes and contrast.

  - Header/Nav: [CONFIRMED] – `components/Navigation.tsx`

import React from 'react'; export const About: React.FC = () => { return (
{/* Header Section */}
National Spatial Data Infrastructure

Bridging the Gap in
Spatial Intelligence.

GuyNode is a grassroots, open data initiative designed to democratize access to geospatial information and empower decision-makers across Guyana.

{/* Main Content Grid */}
{/* The Problem & Solution */}

The Challenge

Government websites for Guyana scarcely provide spatial data in GIS-ready formats to the public. Critical maps are often supplied only as static PDFs, JPGs, or TIFFs, which cannot be utilized for meaningful GIS analysis without significant, time-consuming geoprocessing.

GuyNode is the grassroots response to this interoperability crisis.

We systematically collect GIS layers and maps about Guyana, standardize them into open formats, and host them on a single, unified platform. Our mission is to ensure this data remains freely accessible to the entire geospatial community.

{/* Value Proposition */}

Why GuyNode Matters

Spatial data downloaded from the GuyNode portal serves as the foundational base layer for critical national activities. By standardizing access, we enable:

{[ "Academic Research & Education", "Infrastructure Monitoring", "Flood Management & Defense", "Disease Vector Mapping", "Crime Pattern Analysis", "Climate Vulnerability Assessments" ].map((item, i) => (
{item}
))}
{/* Data Sources */}

Our Data Portfolio

We provide direct downloads or secure links to external repositories. All datasets stored on GuyNode are strictly Public Domain.

  • Primary Datasets Administrative boundaries (RDCs, Municipalities, Villages), Digital Elevation Models (DEMs), Soils, Vegetation, Sea Defenses, and Forestry data.
  • Trusted Sources Bureau of Statistics, Guyana Lands & Surveys Commission, Open Street Map, World Bank, FAO, and US Government Archives.
  • Digitization Efforts Select shapefiles have been meticulously digitized from public-domain documents to preserve historical accuracy.
{/* Stewardship & Maintenance (New) */}

Stewardship

GuyNode is maintained by a dedicated project team committed to open data principles. We are not an official government entity but act as stewards for public-domain geospatial data.

Updates are released incrementally to ensure stability. If you spot an error in the data or the platform, please help us improve by reporting it.

Report an Issue →
{/* Partnerships */}

Map Sharing Partnerships

We are building a community. If you possess map layers pertaining to Guyana (Schools, Healthcare Facilities, Drainage, Rice Lands, etc.) and wish to share them with the public, we will host them with full attribution to you.

); };

import React, { useEffect } from 'react'; import { ViewState } from '../types'; interface SupportPageProps { navigate: (view: ViewState, params?: any) => void; section?: string; } export const SupportPage: React.FC = ({ navigate, section }) => { useEffect(() => { if (section) { const element = document.getElementById(section); if (element) { // Add a slight delay to ensure render is stable setTimeout(() => { element.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'start' }); }, 100); } } else { window.scrollTo(0, 0); } }, [section]); return (
{/* Header */}

Support & Legal

Resources for using GuyNode data responsibly, understanding your rights, and reporting platform issues.

{/* 1. Data License */}

Data License & Usage

GuyNode operates as an Open Geospatial Data Hub. Unless otherwise noted in specific dataset metadata, all data is provided under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0) license or is in the Public Domain.

You are free to:

  • Share: Copy and redistribute the material in any medium or format.
  • Adapt: Remix, transform, and build upon the material for any purpose, even commercially.

Under the following terms:

  • Attribution: You must give appropriate credit to "GuyNode" and the original Source Authority (e.g., "Source: GuyNode / Guyana Lands & Surveys Commission"), provide a link to the license, and indicate if changes were made.

Note: This platform aggregates data from various agencies. While we standardize formats, the original copyright may reside with the producing agency (e.g., GLSC, Bureau of Statistics). Always check the specific metadata panel for each layer.

{/* 2. Privacy Policy */}

Privacy Policy

GuyNode is built with a "Privacy First" architecture. We do not track individual user behavior for advertising purposes.

  • No Tracking: We do not use third-party advertising cookies or cross-site trackers.
  • Local Data: Preferences (such as Theme selection or Admin session tokens) are stored in your browser's `localStorage` and never transmitted to our servers except to maintain session state.
  • AI Processing: When using the AI Assistant, your query is sent to Google Gemini APIs for processing. No personal identifiers are attached to these requests by GuyNode.
{/* 3. Terms of Service */}

Terms of Service

1. Disclaimer of Warranty

The data is provided "as is" without warranty of any kind, express or implied, including but not limited to the warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, and non-infringement.

2. Limitation of Liability

In no event shall the authors, contributors, or GuyNode maintainers be liable for any claim, damages, or other liability, whether in an action of contract, tort, or otherwise, arising from, out of, or in connection with the software or the use or other dealings in the data.

⚠️
Critical Notice regarding Navigation & Land Disputes:
GuyNode data is for informational and planning purposes only. It should NOT be used as the sole basis for:
  • Legal land boundary disputes.
  • Real-time navigation (air, sea, or land).
  • Critical safety infrastructure engineering without ground-truthing.
Always consult official certified survey plans from the Guyana Lands and Surveys Commission (GLSC) for legal determinations.
{/* 4. Report Issue */}

Contact & Support

Found a broken link, missing file, or map error? Let us know so we can fix it for the community.

**Public Changelog**

{
  "area": "Accessibility & Data",
  "version": "unreleased",
  "title": "Improved accessibility and dataset metadata",
  "summary": "We've improved keyboard navigation and added clearer source and update information to all dataset cards in the catalog.",
  "breakingChange": false
}
```

### Verification Checklist

- [ ] “Skip to main content” link appears on focus (top left) and jumps to main content.

- [ ] Catalog cards now display "Source:" and "Updated:" text clearly.

- [ ] Report Issue page clarifies that it handles technical/data issues, not legal disputes.
- [ ] Support/Legal page footer links correctly to Report Issue for contact.
- [ ] No new runtime AI calls were introduced.

GUYNODE TASK: SECURITY POSTURE, ROADMAP HOOKS, AND OLD/NEW COEXISTENCE (EXECUTION MODE)

You are operating under the “GUYNODE MASTER INSTRUCTION FILE (v3)” as the sole protocol for this project. Obey that protocol first, then this task.

MODE:
- You are in EXECUTION mode for this task.
- You ARE allowed to modify code and content, but ONLY in service of:
  - Clarifying the security posture appropriate for a front-end-only SDI portal,
  - Embedding lightweight roadmap hooks into the UI (where appropriate),
  - Supporting a clear coexistence/deprecation plan between the old and new Guynode sites.
- You MUST obey all architectural and logging rules in the Master Instruction File and Guardian Layer.
- You MUST NOT introduce new auth systems, backend infrastructure, or runtime AI calls. This task is about messaging, structure, and configuration guidance only.

ASSUMPTIONS ABOUT CONTEXT:
- The code provided below includes, in full or in part, the relevant modules for:
  - App shell / layout / navigation (including any banners or site-wide messaging),
  - About page and/or any “Project info”/“What is Guynode” pages,
  - Support/Legal pages (Data License, Privacy Policy, Terms of Service),
  - Admin/Dev tools pages (Internal Dev Log, Changelog, Triage Library, QA checklist),
  - Forms such as “Request a demo” and “Report an issue”.
- You CANNOT access the filesystem. The pasted code is your entire world.
- You MUST NOT ask for file paths or filenames. Infer roles from imports, exports, component names, JSX structure, and content.

---------------------------------------------
TASK 0 – ROLE LOCATION & CONFIDENCE MAPPING
---------------------------------------------
From the provided code, identify modules relevant to EACH of the three focus areas and label your confidence.

0.1 Identify roles

A) Security posture (front-end-only)
   - About/support/FAQ-style content that describes Guynode,
   - Support/Legal pages (License, Privacy, ToS),

B) Roadmap hooks (future features, v1.x/v2)

C) Old–new coexistence & deprecation
   - App shell / layout / navigation that could show:

   - Public Changelog or “Status” components.

0.2 Confidence labeling

For each role, label identified modules as:

- [CONFIRMED] – clearly implements that role.
- [GUESSED] – plausible but ambiguous.
- [MISSING] – no implementation visible in the provided code.

0.3 Mapping report

Output a concise mapping like:

- Roadmap hooks:

- Old–new coexistence:

SAFETY GATE:
- You may only MODIFY files whose roles are [CONFIRMED].
- For [GUESSED]/[MISSING] roles:
  - Do NOT modify those files.
  - Instead, describe what you WOULD change there, marked “Requires human confirmation / additional files”.

---------------------------------------------------
TASK 1 – SECURITY POSTURE (FRONT-END-ONLY CLARITY)
---------------------------------------------------
Goal:
- Make the UI honest and clear about Guynode’s security posture:
  - No login, no accounts,
  - Minimal user data collected (if any),
  - Admin/Dev tools are NOT a secure admin console,
  - Forms are for contact/feedback, not sensitive information.

Scope:
- Operate only on [CONFIRMED] About/Support/Legal pages, Admin/Dev tools, and user input forms.

1.1 Minimal change plan (Security posture)
In 4–8 bullet points, describe how you will:

- Ensure About/Support/Legal clearly:
  - State that Guynode is a front-end spatial data portal,
  - Does not provide user accounts or store sensitive personal data by design.
- Clarify form usage:
  - “Request a demo” and “Report an issue” should:
    - Explain what kind of information users should (and should NOT) send,
    - Suggest not including sensitive data (passwords, ID numbers, etc.).
- Clarify Admin/Dev tools:
  - Add a visible note at the top of Admin/Dev pages stating:
    - “Developer tools – not a secure admin panel. Do not store sensitive or personal data here.”
- Align Support/Legal copy:

    - No login/user account system,

    - How contact forms are handled at a high level, without overpromising.

1.2 Implementation (Security posture)
- In About/Support/Legal components:
  - Add a short “Security & Privacy posture” paragraph where appropriate (e.g. About, Privacy Policy) confirming:
    - The app is a public data portal without user accounts,
    - Users should avoid sending sensitive personal data via forms,

- In Admin/Dev tools components (Internal Dev Log, Changelog, Triage Library, QA checklist pages):
  - Add a banner or lead paragraph explaining:
    - These are developer tools,
    - They are not a hardened admin interface,

- In user input forms:
  - Add a one-line note near the form intro or submit button indicating:
    - “Please do not include sensitive personal information in this message.”
  - Ensure this copy is neutral and non-alarming.

Important:
- Do NOT claim encryption or security guarantees that are not visibly implemented.
- Keep all text factual, modest, and consistent with a static SDI front-end.

------------------------------
TASK 2 – ROADMAP HOOKS (FUTURE)
------------------------------
Goal:

2.2 Implementation (Roadmap hooks)

-----------------------------------------------

-----------------------------------------------
Goal:

- Provide textual guidance (NOT actual infra config) for:
  - Redirecting old URLs to new views (e.g., data categories → Catalog filters, old map pages → Viewer with preselected layers).
- Ensure any new messaging:
  - Doesn’t imply the old site is instantly gone (unless that’s already the case),

3.2 Implementation (Coexistence & deprecation)
- In app shell/layout or a “status”/changelog-related component:
  - Add a small, dismissible (or simply unobtrusive) text block stating:
    - That this interface is a newer version of Guynode,
    - That improvements are ongoing,
    - That feedback via “Report an issue” is welcome.
- Provide plain-text guidance snippets (not executable config) for deployment, such as:
  - Example mappings:
    - “Old /data/mining-concessions → Catalog with ‘Mining’ filter and preselected dataset in Viewer”
    - “Old /maps/guyana-basemap → Viewer with base map only”.
  - If appropriate, a small suggested section of `_redirects` or `netlify.toml` in comments, clearly labeled as “deployment-time suggestion, not part of the React app”.

Important:
- Do NOT hard-code environment-specific hostnames or paths beyond generic examples.
- Do NOT implement runtime redirects in the SPA unless that pattern is already used and safe.

---------------------------------------
TASK 4 – LOGGING MANDATE COMPLIANCE
---------------------------------------
Per the Guynode Master Instruction File, you must provide logging outputs for this change. Do NOT modify log files directly; output entries as plain text.

4.2 Public Changelog entries (text only)
Provide one or more `PublicChangelogEntry`-style objects with:

- area: "Support", "Roadmap", or "Deployment" (as appropriate),
- version: "unreleased" (unless a specific version is clearly indicated),
- title: short, user-facing description (e.g. "Clarified security & support information"),

-----------------------------
TASK 5 – OUTPUT FORMAT
-----------------------------
Your response MUST contain, in this order:

1) Role Mapping & Confidence

2) Plans

   - Only modify [CONFIRMED] files.

4) Logging Entries
   - Internal Dev Log entry/entries (plain-text objects),

5) Verification Checklist
   - A concise list of manual checks, including at least:

     - [ ] About/Support/Legal pages clearly state that Guynode is a front-end spatial data portal without user accounts.
     - [ ] Admin/Dev tools pages show a clear “Developer tools – not a secure admin panel” disclaimer.
     - [ ] “Request a demo” and “Report an issue” pages include a brief note asking users not to send sensitive personal information.
     - [ ] A small “Roadmap” or “Future improvements” section exists in a sensible place (e.g. About or Developers), and does not promise specific dates or guarantees.

     - [ ] Suggested redirect/legacy URL mapping guidance is present as plain text only (no accidental runtime redirect loops).

Remember:

import React from 'react'; export const About: React.FC = () => { return (
{/* Header Section */}
National Spatial Data Infrastructure

Bridging the Gap in
Spatial Intelligence.

GuyNode is a grassroots, open data initiative designed to democratize access to geospatial information and empower decision-makers across Guyana.

{/* Main Content Grid */}
{/* The Problem & Solution */}

The Challenge

Government websites for Guyana scarcely provide spatial data in GIS-ready formats to the public. Critical maps are often supplied only as static PDFs, JPGs, or TIFFs, which cannot be utilized for meaningful GIS analysis without significant, time-consuming geoprocessing.

GuyNode is the grassroots response to this interoperability crisis.

We systematically collect GIS layers and maps about Guyana, standardize them into open formats, and host them on a single, unified platform. Our mission is to ensure this data remains freely accessible to the entire geospatial community.

{/* Value Proposition */}

Why GuyNode Matters

Spatial data downloaded from the GuyNode portal serves as the foundational base layer for critical national activities. By standardizing access, we enable:

{[ "Academic Research & Education", "Infrastructure Monitoring", "Flood Management & Defense", "Disease Vector Mapping", "Crime Pattern Analysis", "Climate Vulnerability Assessments" ].map((item, i) => (
{item}
))}
{/* Data Sources */}

Our Data Portfolio

We provide direct downloads or secure links to external repositories. All datasets stored on GuyNode are strictly Public Domain.

  • Primary Datasets Administrative boundaries (RDCs, Municipalities, Villages), Digital Elevation Models (DEMs), Soils, Vegetation, Sea Defenses, and Forestry data.
  • Trusted Sources Bureau of Statistics, Guyana Lands & Surveys Commission, Open Street Map, World Bank, FAO, and US Government Archives.
  • Digitization Efforts Select shapefiles have been meticulously digitized from public-domain documents to preserve historical accuracy.
{/* Stewardship & Maintenance */}

Stewardship

GuyNode is maintained by a dedicated project team committed to open data principles. We are not an official government entity but act as stewards for public-domain geospatial data.

Updates are released incrementally to ensure stability. If you spot an error in the data or the platform, please help us improve by reporting it.

Report an Issue →
{/* Privacy & Architecture (New) */}

Architecture & Privacy

Guynode is architected as a Static Front-End Portal. This means:

  • No User Accounts: We do not maintain a user database. You do not need to log in to access public data.
  • No Personal Tracking: We do not store personal search history or usage profiles associated with individuals.
  • Client-Side Processing: Most data operations (filtering, basic visualization) happen directly in your browser.

We focus on data availability, not data collection.

{/* Partnerships */}

Map Sharing Partnerships

We are building a community. If you possess map layers pertaining to Guyana (Schools, Healthcare Facilities, Drainage, Rice Lands, etc.) and wish to share them with the public, we will host them with full attribution to you.

); };

import React from 'react'; import { DevelopersDatasetTable } from './developers/DevelopersDatasetTable'; export const DevelopersPage: React.FC = () => { return (
{/* Header */}
Developer Resources

Build with GuyNode

Programmatic access to Guyana's National Spatial Data Infrastructure. Integrate verified datasets directly into your applications, analysis workflows, or GIS platforms via our open static API.

{/* Architecture Overview */}

Architecture Overview

GuyNode operates as a Static Geospatial Hub. This means we do not gate data behind complex authentication flows or rate-limited REST APIs.

Datasets are served as optimized, static files (GeoJSON, CSV) via a global CDN. This ensures high availability, low latency, and ease of integration for any client-side or server-side language.

Your App
HTTP GET
CDN
Direct File Access • No Auth Headers • CORS Enabled
{/* Dataset Endpoints */}

Dataset Endpoints

{/* Integration Guides */}

Integration Guides

{/* Leaflet */}
🗺️

Web Mapping (Leaflet/Mapbox)

Consume GeoJSON endpoints directly via fetch or vector tile services. All endpoints support CORS.

L.geoJSON(data).addTo(map);
{/* Python */}
🐍

Python & Data Science

Streamline analysis with GeoPandas. Direct read support allows for reproducible notebooks without local file management.

gdf = gpd.read_file(url)
{/* QGIS */}
🖥️

Desktop GIS (QGIS/ArcGIS)

Add layers via "Add Vector Layer" > "Protocol (HTTPS)". Copy the GeoJSON URL from the table above.

Source Type: Protocol
{/* Metadata & Standards */}

Metadata & Standards

Guynode enforces strict metadata standards to ensure interoperability across the ecosystem.

  • CRS All GeoJSON outputs are transformed to WGS84 (EPSG:4326).
  • Encoding UTF-8 character encoding is mandatory for all attribute tables.
  • Topology Boundaries are simplified for web performance. High-precision files available on request.

Example Metadata Object

{`{
  "type": "Feature",
  "properties": {
    "region_id": "GY-10",
    "region_name": "Upper Demerara-Berbice",
    "population_2012": 39992,
    "last_updated": "2024-01-15T00:00:00Z"
  },
  "geometry": { ... }
}`}
{/* Future Roadmap (New) */}

Future Capabilities Planned

We are continuously improving the developer experience. The following features are on our exploration roadmap for future iterations:

Vector Tiles (MVT)

Serving heavy layers (e.g. detailed hydrology) as Mapbox Vector Tiles for better client-side performance.

Advanced Place Finder

Expanded gazetteer support for locating informal settlements and historic place names.

OGC Standards

Evaluation of WMS/WFS endpoints for direct integration with legacy enterprise GIS software.

{/* Contribution & License */}

Technical Contributions

We welcome submissions from the GIS community. If you maintain a high-value dataset for Guyana, we can host it.

  • • Accepted Formats: GeoJSON, Shapefile (zipped), KML
  • • Metadata Required: Source, Year, License

Licensing & Usage

Unless otherwise noted, data on Guynode is Public Domain or Open Data (CC-BY). We aggregate from official sources (GLSC, Bureau of Statistics) and global open repositories (OSM).

Disclaimer: While we validate data integrity, Guynode is not the legal authority for land disputes. Use official survey plans for legal boundaries.

); };

import React, { useEffect } from 'react'; import { ViewState } from '../types'; interface SupportPageProps { navigate: (view: ViewState, params?: any) => void; section?: string; } export const SupportPage: React.FC = ({ navigate, section }) => { useEffect(() => { if (section) { const element = document.getElementById(section); if (element) { // Add a slight delay to ensure render is stable setTimeout(() => { element.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'start' }); }, 100); } } else { window.scrollTo(0, 0); } }, [section]); return (
{/* Header */}

Support & Legal

Resources for using GuyNode data responsibly, understanding your rights, and reporting platform issues.

{/* 1. Data License */}

Data License & Usage

GuyNode operates as an Open Geospatial Data Hub. Unless otherwise noted in specific dataset metadata, all data is provided under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0) license or is in the Public Domain.

You are free to:

  • Share: Copy and redistribute the material in any medium or format.
  • Adapt: Remix, transform, and build upon the material for any purpose, even commercially.

Under the following terms:

  • Attribution: You must give appropriate credit to "GuyNode" and the original Source Authority (e.g., "Source: GuyNode / Guyana Lands & Surveys Commission"), provide a link to the license, and indicate if changes were made.

Note: This platform aggregates data from various agencies. While we standardize formats, the original copyright may reside with the producing agency (e.g., GLSC, Bureau of Statistics). Always check the specific metadata panel for each layer.

{/* 2. Privacy Policy */}

Privacy Policy

GuyNode is built with a "Privacy First" architecture. We do not track individual user behavior for advertising purposes.

  • No Tracking: We do not use third-party advertising cookies or cross-site trackers.
  • Local Data: Preferences (such as Theme selection or Admin session tokens) are stored in your browser's `localStorage` and never transmitted to our servers except to maintain session state.
  • AI Processing: When using the AI Assistant, your query is sent to Google Gemini APIs for processing. No personal identifiers are attached to these requests by GuyNode.
  • Forms: Data submitted via the "Report an Issue" or "Request a Demo" forms is used solely for correspondence. Do not submit sensitive personal information (financial data, government IDs) through these channels.
{/* 3. Terms of Service */}

Terms of Service

1. Disclaimer of Warranty

The data is provided "as is" without warranty of any kind, express or implied, including but not limited to the warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, and non-infringement.

2. Limitation of Liability

In no event shall the authors, contributors, or GuyNode maintainers be liable for any claim, damages, or other liability, whether in an action of contract, tort, or otherwise, arising from, out of, or in connection with the software or the use or other dealings in the data.

⚠️
Critical Notice regarding Navigation & Land Disputes:
GuyNode data is for informational and planning purposes only. It should NOT be used as the sole basis for:
  • Legal land boundary disputes.
  • Real-time navigation (air, sea, or land).
  • Critical safety infrastructure engineering without ground-truthing.
Always consult official certified survey plans from the Guyana Lands and Surveys Commission (GLSC) for legal determinations.
{/* 4. Report Issue */}

Contact & Support

Found a broken link, missing file, or map error? Let us know so we can fix it for the community.

); };

## Deployment Model
- Type: Single Page Application (SPA)
- Hosting: Static (Netlify/Vercel/S3)
- Routing: Client-side via Query Parameters (Hash/History API fallback handled by App.tsx)

## Environment Variables
- NODE_ENV: Set to 'production' for builds to disable debug logging and hide error stack traces.
- API_KEY: Required for AI features (Gemini).

## Robots.txt Configuration
User-agent: *
Allow: /
Disallow: /?view=ADMIN_ACCESS
Disallow: /?view=INTERNAL_LOG
Sitemap: https://guynode.gy/sitemap.xml

## Sitemap.xml Structure (Static Generation Recommended)
- https://guynode.gy/
- https://guynode.gy/?view=CATALOG
- https://guynode.gy/?view=MAP
- https://guynode.gy/?view=BLOG_INDEX
- https://guynode.gy/?view=ABOUT
- https://guynode.gy/?view=DOCS

## Build Notes
- External GeoJSON URLs in the catalog may require CORS proxies if they do not send Access-Control-Allow-Origin headers.
- The Admin Console is hidden behind a client-side soft gate. Do not rely on it for securing sensitive data in production.

## Migration & Redirect Strategy
(Recommended for replacing legacy sites)

If deploying this SPA to replace an existing multi-page site, configure your host (Netlify _redirects or Vercel config) to map old paths to new query params.

Examples:
- Old: /data/mining-concessions.html
  New: /?view=CATALOG&category=Economy

- Old: /maps/viewer.php
  New: /?view=MAP

- Old: /about-us
  New: /?view=ABOUT

Ensure "Beta" messaging is active in App.tsx during the transition period.

If the Viewer sidebar AI section is [CONFIRMED]:

- Remove:
  - The “AI Insight” / “Generate AI Summary” UI block,
  - Any event handlers or hooks that call AI helper functions,
  - Any AI-related imports.
- Ensure:
  - The sidebar still shows dataset info, layer controls, etc.,
  - Layout remains functional and readable.

2.6 Support/Privacy content

If the Support/Privacy component (e.g. `SupportPage.tsx` or privacy-related content) is [CONFIRMED]:

- Remove or rewrite any references that suggest live AI processing of user data.
  - Example: remove “Your data may be processed by AI for summaries…” if present.
- Replace with accurate copy, such as:
  - “The Guynode site does not use live AI services at runtime. Any AI-assisted tooling is used during development only and does not process your data while you browse the site.”
- Keep the copy modest and truthful; do not imply features that don’t exist.

2.7 Deployment notes / docs

If deployment notes (e.g. `docs/deployment_notes.txt`) are [CONFIRMED]:

- Remove AI-specific deployment instructions:
  - API keys for Gemini or similar,
  - Steps required to enable AI features.
- Optionally:
  - Add a short note that:
    - “Runtime AI features were removed for v1; no AI API keys are required for deployment.”

2.8 Clean up imports and dead code

- In all [CONFIRMED] modules, after you remove AI usage:
  - Remove any now-unused imports (including AI helpers, types, and React hooks used only by AI features).
  - Ensure there are no references to removed functions.
- Do NOT remove unrelated functionality.

====================================================
TASK 3 – ALIGNMENT CHECK & SAFETY SUMMARY
====================================================
Goal:
- Confirm that the resulting codebase aligns with “no runtime AI by default” and that no AI-related runtime paths remain.

3.1 Alignment summary

Provide a clear narrative summarizing:

- Whether:
  - All AI library imports (`@google/genai` etc.) have been removed from runtime code and HTML entry.
  - All AI helper functions have been removed or neutralized.
  - All AI-triggering UI elements (Assistant, AI Summary buttons, AI Insight sections) have been removed.
- Confirm that:
  - The app no longer requires any AI API key to run.
  - There are no intentional runtime calls to AI endpoints.

3.2 Residual risk (if any)

If any AI-related code or text remains (e.g. intentionally archived helper file):

- Call it out explicitly.
- Justify why it is inert (e.g. “never imported anywhere; no references; safe to delete in a future cleanup”).

====================================================
TASK 4 – LOGGING MANDATE COMPLIANCE
====================================================
Per the Guynode Master Instruction File, you must provide logging outputs for this change. Do NOT modify log files directly; output entries as plain text.

4.1 Internal Dev Log entry/entries (text only)

Provide one or more `InternalDevLogEntry`-style objects with:

- area: "AI Decommissioning"
- severity: "info"
- title: e.g. "Removed runtime Gemini integration and AI UI triggers"
- summary: 1–3 sentences summarizing the work.
- details: bullet list describing:
  - Which modules were updated (HTML entry, AI helper, Assistant, Catalog cards, Viewer sidebar, Support/Privacy, docs),
  - Which AI libraries/imports were removed,
  - Which UI elements were removed or simplified,
  - Any inert/archived AI-related files left in place with no imports.

4.2 Public Changelog entry (text only)

Provide a `PublicChangelogEntry`-style object with:

- area: "Architecture/AI"
- version: "unreleased" (unless a specific version is clearly indicated)
- title: e.g. "Removed experimental AI features from the Guynode interface"
- summary: 1–2 user-facing sentences such as:
  - “Removed experimental AI-powered summaries and assistant features to keep the Guynode portal lightweight and focused on stable data access.”
- breakingChange: false

====================================================
TASK 5 – OUTPUT FORMAT
====================================================
Your response MUST contain, in this order:

1) Role Mapping & Confidence
   - The full mapping from Task 0 with [CONFIRMED]/[GUESSED]/[MISSING].

2) Minimal Change Plan
   - The bullet list from Task 1.1 describing the planned changes.

3) Updated Code
   - Full, updated contents for EACH [CONFIRMED] module you actually modified:
     - HTML entry (e.g. `index.html`) if changed,
     - AI helper/service module,
     - Assistant UI component,
     - Catalog card component,
     - Viewer sidebar component,
     - Support/Privacy component,
     - Deployment notes docs (if included in the provided code).
   - Only modify [CONFIRMED] modules.
   - Do NOT output snippets; output full file contents for each modified module.
   - Clearly separate different files with headings/labels.

4) Alignment Summary
   - The narrative from Task 3.1–3.2 confirming no runtime AI usage remains and describing any inert leftovers.

5) Logging Entries
   - Internal Dev Log entry/entries (plain-text objects),
   - Public Changelog entry (plain-text object).

6) Verification Checklist
   - A concise checklist, including at least:

     - [ ] App builds successfully with no references to `@google/genai` (or similar AI libraries).
     - [ ] App runs in the browser without any AI-related runtime errors or missing API keys.
     - [ ] No “Assistant” UI is visible anywhere in the app.
     - [ ] No “Generate AI Summary” or “AI Insight” buttons/sections appear in Catalog or Viewer.
     - [ ] Network tab (DevTools) shows no requests to AI/LLM endpoints when using the app.
     - [ ] Support/Privacy/Help text no longer claims that live AI is processing user data during normal browsing.
     - [ ] Deployment notes no longer reference AI API keys as required for deployment.
     - [ ] No new AI-related code, dependencies, or runtime network calls were introduced as part of this change.

Remember:
- Modify ONLY [CONFIRMED] modules.
- Do NOT introduce new AI usage of any kind.
- Keep the changes minimal, explicit, and fully aligned with the “no runtime AI by default” goal for Guynode v1.

**Prioritizing Feature Removal**

I've been meticulously reviewing the codebase and relevant documentation. My primary focus is identifying and isolating all components directly related to runtime AI/LLM functionality. The goal is a clean removal, leaving a stable and functional core. This requires a precise understanding of dependencies and a carefully orchestrated unlinking of affected modules. The work is proceeding efficiently.


**Analyzing Dependencies Deeply**

I've pinpointed every instance of AI/LLM integration. Confirmed that `metadata.json` requests microphone access and I'll remove that. The import map in `index.html` has a `@google/genai` entry I'll eliminate.  I'll either replace `geminiService.ts` with a stub or a no-op implementation and carefully assess its imports. The goal is the smallest possible codebase change to achieve the removal goals.


**Finalizing Code Cleanup**

I'm removing the AI/LLM components, starting with `App.tsx`, `CatalogCard.tsx`, and `ViewerSidebar.tsx`, and will stub `geminiService.ts`. I'm replacing the Assistant component with null, and I will ensure the Privacy Policy is updated.


**Neutralizing AI Presence**

I'm systematically removing all traces of AI functionality. I've eliminated `requestFramePermissions` from `metadata.json` and removed the `@google/genai` reference from `index.html`. In `App.tsx`, I've removed the `Assistant` import and JSX element.  I neutralized the `Assistant` component by replacing its content with `null`, while `geminiService.ts` now features stubbed methods, which will remove all of its AI capabilities. This is making great progress.


**Orchestrating AI Removal**

I'm systematically removing all remaining AI-related elements. I've targeted `CatalogCard.tsx` and `ViewerSidebar.tsx`, removing their AI-specific imports and UI components. Next, I'll modify `SupportPage.tsx` and `deployment_notes.txt` to reflect the changes. I'll thoroughly revise `geminiService.ts`, ensuring it no longer relies on the removed `@google/genai` import and related implementation. I will continue to follow my plan, which I confirmed is still valid.


**Executing Targeted Refactors**

I'm now in the process of implementing the code changes according to my outlined plan. I've updated `metadata.json`, removed the `@google/genai` reference from `index.html`, and replaced the `geminiService.ts` with the stubbed functions as described. Additionally, I have successfully replaced the `Assistant` component with `null` as planned. The remaining modules requiring updates are next on the list.


**Executing Final Code Removal**

I'm systematically removing all remaining AI-related elements. I've updated `App.tsx`, `CatalogCard.tsx`, and `ViewerSidebar.tsx`, eliminating any AI-specific imports and UI components. Next, I'll modify `SupportPage.tsx` and `deployment_notes.txt` to reflect these changes. I'll also double-check imports to ensure all dependencies are resolved. This feels good.

**1) Role Mapping & Confidence**

- **HTML entry**: [CONFIRMED] – `index.html` (Contains import map with `@google/genai`).
- **AI helper/service**: [CONFIRMED] – `services/geminiService.ts`.
- **Assistant UI**: [CONFIRMED] – `components/Assistant.tsx`.
- **Catalog card with AI button**: [CONFIRMED] – `components/CatalogCard.tsx`.
- **Viewer sidebar with AI section**: [CONFIRMED] – `components/viewer/ViewerSidebar.tsx`.
- **Support/Privacy with AI mention**: [CONFIRMED] – `components/SupportPage.tsx`.
- **Deployment notes/docs with AI key**: [CONFIRMED] – `docs/deployment_notes.txt`.
- **App shell/root where Assistant is rendered**: [CONFIRMED] – `App.tsx`.
- **Metadata config**: [CONFIRMED] – `metadata.json` (Requests microphone permission).

**2) Minimal Change Plan**

*   **`metadata.json`**: Remove the `requestFramePermissions` array to drop microphone access requirements.
*   **`index.html`**: Remove the `@google/genai` dependency from the `importmap`.
*   **`services/geminiService.ts`**: Remove the `@google/genai` import and all internal logic. Replace exported functions (`generateAIResponse`, `summarizeDataset`) with safe no-op stubs to prevent build errors in case of lingering references.
*   **`components/Assistant.tsx`**: Neutralize the component to return `null` and remove internal logic/state, ensuring no runtime code executes.
*   **`App.tsx`**: Remove the `Assistant` import and the `<Assistant />` element from the render tree.
*   **`components/CatalogCard.tsx`**: Remove the "Generate AI Summary" button, the `summary` state, and the import from `geminiService`.
*   **`components/viewer/ViewerSidebar.tsx`**: Remove the "AI Insight" section, the `summary` state, and the import from `geminiService`.
*   **`components/SupportPage.tsx`**: Update the Privacy Policy section to remove references to AI processing of user data.
*   **`docs/deployment_notes.txt`**: Remove the requirement for `API_KEY` and the `NODE_ENV` note about AI features.

**3) Updated Code**

{/* Header */}

Support & Legal

Resources for using GuyNode data responsibly, understanding your rights, and reporting platform issues.

{/* 1. Data License */}

Data License & Usage

GuyNode operates as an Open Geospatial Data Hub. Unless otherwise noted in specific dataset metadata, all data is provided under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0) license or is in the Public Domain.

You are free to:

  • Share: Copy and redistribute the material in any medium or format.
  • Adapt: Remix, transform, and build upon the material for any purpose, even commercially.

Under the following terms:

  • Attribution: You must give appropriate credit to "GuyNode" and the original Source Authority (e.g., "Source: GuyNode / Guyana Lands & Surveys Commission"), provide a link to the license, and indicate if changes were made.

Note: This platform aggregates data from various agencies. While we standardize formats, the original copyright may reside with the producing agency (e.g., GLSC, Bureau of Statistics). Always check the specific metadata panel for each layer.

{/* 2. Privacy Policy */}

Privacy Policy

GuyNode is built with a "Privacy First" architecture. We do not track individual user behavior for advertising purposes.

  • No Tracking: We do not use third-party advertising cookies or cross-site trackers.
  • Local Data: Preferences (such as Theme selection or Admin session tokens) are stored in your browser's `localStorage` and never transmitted to our servers except to maintain session state.
  • No AI Runtime: The Guynode site does not use live AI services at runtime. Any AI-assisted tooling is used during development only and does not process your data while you browse the site.
  • Forms: Data submitted via the "Report an Issue" or "Request a Demo" forms is used solely for correspondence. Do not submit sensitive personal information (financial data, government IDs) through these channels.
{/* 3. Terms of Service */}

Terms of Service

1. Disclaimer of Warranty

The data is provided "as is" without warranty of any kind, express or implied, including but not limited to the warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, and non-infringement.

2. Limitation of Liability

In no event shall the authors, contributors, or GuyNode maintainers be liable for any claim, damages, or other liability, whether in an action of contract, tort, or otherwise, arising from, out of, or in connection with the software or the use or other dealings in the data.

⚠️
Critical Notice regarding Navigation & Land Disputes:
GuyNode data is for informational and planning purposes only. It should NOT be used as the sole basis for:
  • Legal land boundary disputes.
  • Real-time navigation (air, sea, or land).
  • Critical safety infrastructure engineering without ground-truthing.
Always consult official certified survey plans from the Guyana Lands and Surveys Commission (GLSC) for legal determinations.
{/* 4. Report Issue */}

Contact & Support

Found a broken link, missing file, or map error? Let us know so we can fix it for the community.
